mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings declines over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 52% in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, which compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 58% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading falling below 3.0% confirms inflationary pressures are subsiding, allowing markets to price in imminent policy easing and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL drops below 3.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates signals structural labor market weakness rather than cyclical fluctuation, leading to sustained earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns with > 55% accuracy, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent single-series effects in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands corporate revenue bases and improves equity valuations, producing consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon following each data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts alongside rising retail sales during an early-cycle expansion phase signals robust consumer confidence and capital expenditure, driving immediate positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST and RSAFS acceleration during early-cycle phases fails to generate positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of historical instances, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 4%.
